steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion: "Since these broadsides became associated with the innovative aesthetics of the Black Arts Movement (1965–1975)." The key context is the phrase "may likewise seem to have been pioneering" immediately after, followed by a correction: "but, in fact" broadside poetry long predates Randall. The underlined portion must be setting up that false impression.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It undermines the argument that Randall's method for publishing poetry was a largely novel one.': Wrong direction. The underlined portion is what creates the impression of novelty; the correction comes after it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It identifies a reason for a possible misconception regarding Randall's publication of broadsides.': Yes. Associating the broadsides with the innovative Black Arts Movement is exactly why a reader might mistakenly think the format was Randall's innovation. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It explains why Randall's broadsides were important to the Black Arts Movement.': The underlined portion notes an association, not an explanation of importance or influence on the movement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It provides context for Randall's decision to publish work by Black poets as broadsides.': The opening sentence already gave that context (making Black poets' work widely available). The underlined portion does something different. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
